weather.tip: Overcast evening at 23.4°C with a moderate 35% rain chance — not a soaking risk, but worth keeping an umbrella handy if you're heading out for a walk or dinner tonight.

around.headline: 5-year-old girl dies after a tree falls on a school bus
around.summary: A 5-year-old girl was killed when a tree fell on the school bus she was travelling in. If you have kids on school transport, it's worth flagging any visibly damaged or overhanging trees near school routes to the school or BBMP.

around.headline: Suspected LPG cylinder blast injures multiple migrant workers at Bommasandra PG
around.summary: A suspected gas leak set off an explosion at a PG accommodation in Bommasandra, injuring at least eight migrant workers (reports range from eight to ten); windows had reportedly been kept shut to keep mosquitoes out, letting leaked gas build up indoors. See today's street-smart scenario for exactly what to do if you smell gas indoors.

around.headline: BMTC adds five new bus services in Bengaluru
around.summary: BMTC has launched five new bus services across the city. Worth checking BMTC's route list if your usual commute has been underserved — one of the new routes may cut your wait time.

around.headline: Criminal cases booked against 249 guest lecturers over fake PhD certificates
around.summary: Karnataka authorities have booked criminal cases against 249 guest lecturers who allegedly submitted fake PhD certificates to secure teaching posts. A reminder that academic credentials at any institution can be verified through the awarding university if you're ever in doubt.

around.headline: Karnataka health department: feed pigeons only between 7 am and 9 pm, never from balconies
around.summary: A new health department rule restricts pigeon feeding to daytime hours and bans feeding from balconies, aiming to cut droppings-related health hazards and mess in residential areas. Feeding outside these hours or from a balcony could now attract a fine.

around.headline: Iran claims it destroyed Patriot and THAAD systems at US bases in Jordan and Kuwait
around.summary: Iran says its strikes destroyed US Patriot missile-defence systems and a THAAD radar at bases in Jordan and Kuwait, as the US-Iran conflict stretches into further nights of strikes. Prolonged Gulf tension has historically fed through to petrol and diesel prices within weeks, so it's worth keeping an eye on pump prices.

events.name: India International Travel Mart — Bengaluru Edition
events.note: A three-day travel and tourism expo kicking off tomorrow — useful if you're planning a trip and want to compare deals from multiple operators in one place.

events.name: Global Chess League 2026
events.note: Bengaluru continues to host matches as teams battle it out in this year's league — worth catching if you follow competitive chess.

events.name: Special Onam trains via Bengaluru (Mysuru–Kannur)
events.note: Special trains have been added between Mysuru and Kannur via Bengaluru for the Onam rush — useful if you're planning festival travel to Kerala.

events.name: Bengaluru Poetry Festival — 10th edition
events.note: The city's flagship poetry festival returns for its 10th year — worth watching for the schedule if you're into spoken word or literature.

street_smart.title: You're in a PG or apartment and notice a strong gas smell — the windows have been kept shut to keep mosquitoes out. What do you do first?
street_smart.based_on: Real incident today: a suspected LPG leak caused an explosion at a Bommasandra PG accommodation, injuring at least eight migrant workers; windows had reportedly been kept shut to keep mosquitoes out, letting the gas build up indoors (NDTV / The Hindu / Deccan Herald).
street_smart.situation: The smell is unmistakable near the kitchen area. It's late evening, the room is stuffy because the windows have been shut, and a couple of people are already reaching for the light switch to see better.

choices.text: Stop anyone from touching light switches or fans, turn off the gas cylinder regulator/valve if you can reach it safely, open all doors and windows fully, get everyone outside, and call 101 (fire) or 112 once you're at a safe distance.
choices.feedback: Correct. LPG is heavier than air, so it pools near the floor in a closed room instead of dissipating — cutting the gas supply and ventilating fully removes the fuel source, while evacuating before calling for help keeps everyone clear of a possible ignition. This sequence (cut gas → ventilate → evacuate → call) is what actually prevents an explosion, not just delays one.

choices.text: Flip on the lights or switch on a fan to see better and clear the smell faster.
choices.feedback: This is the trap. Electrical switches and fans can produce a small spark, and that's exactly enough to ignite a room full of accumulated LPG vapour — this is a leading cause of these blasts, including the one reported today. Never operate any electrical switch in a room where you smell gas.

choices.text: Try to locate the leak yourself by sniffing around the cylinder, or use a lighter or matchstick to check where the flame reacts.
choices.feedback: An open flame near a gas leak can trigger the exact explosion you're trying to avoid. If you need to find a leak, use soap water on the connections and watch for bubbles — never fire.

choices.text: Crack open just one window a little and wait for the smell to fade before deciding what to do next.
choices.feedback: Partial ventilation isn't enough — because LPG sinks and pools near the floor, a single window barely clears it. The priority is cutting the gas supply and getting everyone out immediately, not waiting to see if the smell improves.

takeaways: Never touch a light switch, fan, or any electrical appliance in a room where you smell gas — even a tiny spark can ignite pooled LPG vapour.
Cut the gas at the cylinder regulator first, then open doors and windows fully, then evacuate — in that order.
Call 101 (fire) or 112 only once everyone is outside at a safe distance; never use a flame to search for a leak — soap water and bubbles work safely instead.

quiz.q: How many guest lecturers have had criminal cases booked against them in Karnataka for allegedly submitting fake PhD certificates?
quiz.why: The cases were booked after a verification drive uncovered forged doctoral credentials used to secure teaching posts.

quiz.q: Under Karnataka's new health department rule, during what hours are you allowed to feed pigeons?
quiz.why: The rule also bans feeding from balconies, aiming to cut down on droppings-related mess and health hazards in residential buildings.

quiz.q: Which US missile-defence systems does Iran claim to have destroyed at bases in Jordan and Kuwait?
quiz.why: The claim came as the US-Iran conflict stretched into further consecutive nights of strikes, with Houthi forces separately targeting Gulf shipping.

quiz.q: Why does leaked LPG gas tend to pool near the floor of a room instead of rising to the ceiling?
options: It's colder than the surrounding air
It reacts with oxygen and sinks
quiz.why: This is exactly why ground-level ventilation and cutting the gas supply matter more than just opening a high window during a suspected leak.

quiz.q: What is India's central bank called?
options: Securities and Exchange Board of India
quiz.why: The RBI regulates monetary policy and banking, while SEBI (a different body) regulates the securities markets.
